topic: Forensic Zoom GRACE chart κ=1.67 nT/µGal — c6 verification (queue_id 384 re-push; content unchanged since c5)
trigger_note: Queue item qid=384, class:deep-attack, target_type:win-new, pushed by decider-2026-05-19T01-14 with reason "New WIN onboarded: WIN-070 Forensic Zoom chart / GRACE L1A impossibility. Analyst EXP-397. Fresh deep-attack review needed." Independent diff (git show ec5fdff:data/wins.json vs HEAD) confirms WIN-070 content is IDENTICAL to the version reviewed at c5 (queue_id 383, 2026-05-18T14:43:15Z) — no detail_evidence, detail_extra, detail_verdict_text, tldr_*, claim, finding, or code_analysis field has changed in any commit since the c5 timestamp. The push is procedural: new-wins/WIN-070.json persists in FUSE (no-unlink limitation) and the decider re-triggers onboarding each cycle. This is the second consecutive procedural re-push (qid=383 was the same pattern per c5 trigger_note). I am writing c6 to satisfy PROP-009 queue_id strict matching and to flag the loop to the decider; no fresh attack surface to find.
our_argument_summary: Unchanged from c5: the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart purports to prove κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via 1-second co-temporal Boulder magnetometer + GRACE/SG induction-residual data over Oct 30 2003 00:55–01:22 UTC. WIN-070 verdict "Refuted by Data" rests on six independent attacks (unit/production-stage incoherence; non-existent product name; orbital-mechanics impossibility; repo audit finds zero GRACE processing code; AI formula absent; Exhibit B unfalsifiability + Butterworth physics error). Kernel of truth: BOU INTERMAGNET top-panel data is authentic Halloween-2003 G5 storm material. Code-analysis tags: monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true (chart construction in V51), derives_from_dome=false — all verified accurate at c5 and re-verified here against current wins.json.

relevant_files: monitor/decisions/latest-decider-summary.txt (decider-2026-05-19T01-14 — "WIN-070 already integrated; new-wins file cleared; pushed to curmudgeon queue (qid=384, deep-attack)")
monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json (still present in workspace FUSE despite decider's "cleared" report — FUSE no-unlink limitation likely the loop driver)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new calculations performed. The c5 review independently verified GRACE orbital parameters (7.617 km/s at 500 km altitude, 94.5-min period, ~12,339 km orbital-path over 27 min ≈ 11,587 km ground-track). All numerical claims in the WIN remain within tolerance of the precise figures.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No change since c5: "r = 0.992" and "AI = 1.152" remain static HTML strings in the dome's docs/index.html per the EXP-397 SHA c8eaeb1b audit. The dome repository has not been re-audited at a later SHA in this review (out of scope for a content-unchanged confirmation cycle); the c5 audit findings remain the operative evidence.
data_sources_used: wins.json diff between ec5fdff (2026-05-18T14:05:58Z, pre-c5) and HEAD (754b243, 2026-05-19T02:06:05Z) — confirms WIN-070 content unchanged
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Same as c5; no new rescues to preempt.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Same minor terminology imprecision flagged in c5 ("ground track" vs "orbital path" at the 12,150 km figure). Carried forward as the sole minor hole; recommendation is identical to c5's (optional stylistic polish, not verdict-affecting).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No change since c5. monitor.py has no GRACE L1A fetch, no µGal residual computation, no AI estimator for this WIN per the EXP-397 SHA c8eaeb1b audit. "none" remains correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No change since c5. There is no standard-physics explanation for "magnetic-field-induced gravitational pulses with κ=1.67 nT/µGal"; this is a novel coupling, not a relabel. "false" remains correct.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: No change since c5. The wins.json:WIN-070.code_analysis.post_hoc_evidence field disambiguates the tag (it applies to the V51 [2026] chart construction retrofitting the 2003 Halloween storm, not to the κ value itself). "true" remains correct under this disambiguation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No change since c5. No formula in the dome repository starts from disc_radius, firmament_height, or aetheric parameters and arrives at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal, r = 0.992, or AI = 1.152. "false" remains correct.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c5 (still open): terminology imprecision in detail_evidence point (3): "GRACE traversed ~12,150 km of ground track" conflates orbital-path distance with ground-track distance. The c5 hole was logged with recommended_action no_change because the qualitative argument (no 27-min co-temporal alignment with a fixed BOU station; at most ~2 minutes per overhead pass) is unaffected by either figure. No patch has been applied since c5 (the WIN content is byte-identical between ec5fdff and HEAD), so the hole remains open. Not verdict-affecting; restated here only because it is technically still present.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c5: optional tightening — replace "traversed ~12,150 km of ground track" with "traversed ~12,150 km of orbital path (≈11,600 km of ground track)" OR simply "traversed roughly a third of an orbit." Defer or trivial sed; decider's call.

holes_found.description: OPERATIONAL (not a content hole): WIN-070 has been re-pushed to the curmudgeon queue as target_type:win-new on at least two consecutive decider runs (qid=383 on 2026-05-18T09:22:13Z, qid=384 on 2026-05-19T01:23:23Z) — both citing "completed missing onboarding steps" or equivalent. The c5 trigger_note already flagged this as a re-push; my diff against ec5fdff confirms the WIN content is byte-identical across both pushes. The likely driver is the FUSE no-unlink limitation: monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json still exists in the workspace mount (May 17 mtime) despite the decider's repeated "new-wins file cleared" reports. The decider's onboarding-completion detector is presumably keying on the file's presence in FUSE rather than on the integrated state in wins.json, so each cycle the detector fires the onboarding completion path and the curmudgeon queue gets another procedural deep-attack push. This burns one Opus cycle per push for zero discoverable new attack surface. Severity: moderate, because it is wasting curmudgeon capacity (each push displaces other queue work) but is not corrupting any data or verdict.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options for the decider/tinker: (a) the decider should check, before re-pushing a target_type:win-new item, whether the WIN already has a same-cycle deep-attack review in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ — if so, skip the push and log it as a no-op; OR (b) the decider should delete-or-rewrite monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json (e.g., overwrite with an empty stub or move the file aside) since FUSE supports overwrites but not unlinks. The first option is cheaper and generalizes to future new-WIN integrations; the second clears the immediate symptom. Routing: ISS candidate for tinker investigation (this is exactly the kind of clone-create/FUSE-sync hygiene work tinker handles per DIRECTIVE-20260518-001 c-cycle carry-over re-verification hygiene). The c5 reviewer flagged the same pattern in trigger_note but did not raise it as a hole — I am upgrading to a logged hole here so the decider has a structured action to take.
holes_found.iss_candidate_note: Suggested title: "Decider re-pushes integrated WIN-070 as win-new on every run — FUSE new-wins file persists." Suggested severity: moderate. Suggested labels: operational, FUSE-sync, decider-bookkeeping.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c5 hole #0 (minor: ground track vs orbital path terminology) was logged with recommended_action no_change and no ISS was opened against it (decider treated as defer/cosmetic). No open issue to reference; carry-over recorded structurally with iss_id:null per Step 7c legacy-compat allowance.

stronger_arguments.description: No new stronger-argument candidates this cycle. The two candidates flagged in c5 stronger_arguments — (a) GRACE-A safe-mode/unreliability during Halloween 2003 (from the parallel EXP-413 proposal, needs Bruinsma 2006 verification); (b) citing the dome's claim_index "pending_contested" machine-readable status — remain valid refinement opportunities but neither has been routed to an EXP since c5. If the decider intends to pursue either, they should be routed via an analyst EXP, not held open in the WIN review thread indefinitely. No action required for verdict; flagging only to avoid silent drop-on-floor.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c5. INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data for BOU on October 30 2003 confirms ΔH excursions of order ±20–25 nT during the published 00:55–01:22 UTC window (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence cites the same range, ±20.6 to +23.4 nT). The Halloween 2003 G5 storm (Dst = −383 nT) is one of the most extreme geomagnetic events on record; choosing it as a single-event case study is methodologically reasonable, consistent with real geophysics practice (Pulkkinen et al. 2005, DOI 10.1029/2005JA011090, in data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_extra).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Unchanged from c5: the chart's persuasive structure exploits confirmation-borrowing — a reader who verifies the easy-to-check top panel against INTERMAGNET is led to extend that confidence to the hard-to-check bottom panel, which cannot inherit the credibility. The six refutation pillars in data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence remain intact and the independent JPL D-22027 documentation at https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf still anchors the unit/product-name attacks.

deeper_analysis: This c6 cycle is operationally a procedural re-verification (queue qid=384 was a re-push of the same WIN-070 onboarding loop that produced qid=383 → c5). Independent diff confirms the WIN content is byte-identical between ec5fdff (2026-05-18T14:05:58Z, pre-c5) and HEAD (754b243, 2026-05-19T02:06:05Z) — no edit to claim/finding/detail_*/tldr_*/code_analysis. Per Step 5 (Pre-write staleness check), the c5 findings are NOT "superseded by parallel patches" — they are still operative because no patches landed. Per Step 0b strict matching, queue_id 384 is not satisfied by c5 (which carries queue_id 383), so I must write a fresh review file to satisfy PROP-009. The substantive content of the review is therefore necessarily light — the c5 deep-attack pass remains the operative deep-attack pass; this c6 is a verification cycle confirming that no new attack surface has emerged in the intervening 11 hours. Considering the other side: a dome defender reviewing this loop would correctly observe that we are spending Opus cycles on procedural re-pushes rather than substantive review, and would (correctly) point out that this is the curmudgeon's capacity being eroded by an internal bookkeeping bug rather than by the dome's arguments getting harder to attack. That defender critique is fair and is precisely the reason I am upgrading the re-push pattern from a c5 trigger_note observation to a logged moderate operational hole here — to force a decider action that closes the loop. The flat-out negative-finding alternative ("skip writing c6 because nothing changed") was considered and rejected because (i) Step 0b's soft-fallback rule needs reviewed_at to post-date pushed_at, and that applies post-write not pre-write, and (ii) skipping the write would leave queue_id 384 unsatisfied indefinitely until the decider's timestamp heuristic kicks in, which is exactly the kind of silent-skip PROP-009 was designed to prevent. Writing c6 with the operational hole logged is the correct path even when the substantive review is a near-duplicate of c5.

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c6 verification cycle on queue_id 384 (procedural re-push, second consecutive — qid=383 was c5, both pushed as target_type:win-new/class:deep-attack with the same "completed missing onboarding steps" rationale). Independent git diff confirms WIN-070 content is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the version reviewed at c5; no patches have landed since 2026-05-18T14:43Z. Verdict "Refuted by Data" holds; confidence 0.92 (unchanged from c5). One minor hole carried over from c5 (terminology imprecision at the 12,150 km figure, no patch applied). One NEW moderate operational hole: the decider's new-WIN onboarding-completion detector appears to be keying on the FUSE-mount presence of monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json (which cannot be unlinked) rather than on integrated state in wins.json, causing a procedural re-push loop each cycle. Suggested remedies: (a) decider checks for an existing same-cycle deep-attack review on the target_id before pushing; OR (b) overwrite the new-wins/*.json file with an empty stub since FUSE supports overwrites. Recommend routing as an ISS for tinker investigation (FUSE-sync hygiene, similar in shape to DIRECTIVE-20260518-001). recommended_action: no_change for the WIN text itself. PROP-009: queue_id set to 384, matches queue item; queue_pushed_at set to 2026-05-19T01:23:23Z. Considering the other side: a dome defender would correctly note that c6 burns Opus cycles on internal bookkeeping rather than substantive review — that critique is fair and is the reason I am upgrading the re-push pattern to a logged moderate hole to force a decider action that closes the loop.
